entry.title: Connectivity within cross-regions and cross-subaccounts
entry.content:
Use case

You have subscribed to SAP Build Apps in EU(Frankfurt) region and SAP Build Process Automation in Singapore region. As per the use case, an application built in SAP Build Apps calls a process in SAP Build Process Automation. Based on the use case, "Subaccount 1" should be the Build Apps subaccount (EU(Frankfurt)) and "Subaccount 2" should be your SAP Build Process Automation subaccount (Singapore).

Prerequisites

  1. Service Key is available for SAP Build Process Automation instance.
  2. Make sure that your process in SAP Build Process Automation, is deployed. You have the trigger with the similar Payload as shown in the image below. You will need this information in the last step while configuring REST API in SAP Build Apps. Note the following information in the notepad, we will need this information in next steps:
  • URL: We will refer to the URL mentioned in the API Trigger popup box as ${API_TRIGGER_SBPA_URL}. This will be used in the destination.
  • definitionId: This will be referred to as ${API_TRIGGER_SBPA_DEFINITIONID}. It will be used in the REST API call.
  • Rest of the payload information: The same structure will be created in the REST API in SAP Build Apps. For example, in this case,

"context":{ "_name":"",

"age":""

}

 

Procedure

This scenario can be achieved in 3 steps as explained below:

  • Setup trust between the 2 subaccounts,
  • Configure destination in Subaccount 1,
  • Configure REST API in SAP Build Apps

Setup trust between the 2 subaccounts

Now, to build the trust follow the steps given in the link below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/connectivity/sap-btp-connectivity-cf/set-up-trust-between-systems

Configure Destination

After the trust has been established, you will need to create the destination. Follow this excellent and highly detailed guide:

https://help.sap.com/docs/connectivity/sap-btp-connectivity-cf/user-propagation-between-cloud-foundry-applications?locale=en-US#loio8ebf60c82a8e4cfc904f441c0c0acd6b__assemble

In destination, fill-in the information as given the help document above and also refer to the points mentioned below for additional tips.

  • URL: ${API_TRIGGER_SBPA_URL}
  • Authentication: OAuth2SAMLBearerAssertion
  • Key Store Location: <blank>
  • Key Store password: <blank>
  • Client Key: clientid to be retrieved from service key created for SAP Build Process Automation instance in Subaccount 2
  • Token Service User: clientid to be retrieved from service key created for SAP Build Process Automation instance in Subaccount 2
  • Token Service Password: clientsecret to be retrieved from service key created for SAP Build Process Automation instance in Subaccount 2

Additional Properties:

  • AppgyverEnabled: true
  • BuildApps.ApiType: true
  • BuildApps.Enabled: true
  • BuildApps.ApiType: rest
  • CEP.EnableCsrfProtection: true
  • HTML5.DynamicDestination: true
  • nameIdFormat: urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:1.1:nameid-format:emailAddress
  • sap.cloud.service: com.sap.spa.processautomation
  • sap.cloud.service.alias: spa
  • WebIDEEnabled: true

Apart from nameIDFormat, make sure to add rest all the other additional parameters in the destination. The connection check for the destination should result in 401: Unauthorized. The destination is now ready.

Configure REST API within SAP Build Apps

Open SAP Build Apps and navigate to Integration > SAP Systems > Add Integration > Choose your destination > Install Integration > Add New REST API Integration. Next steps to follow are described in the below link under section REST API Integration in Build Apps.

https://help.sap.com/docs/build-apps/service-guide/sap-build-process-automation#rest-api-integration-in-sap-build-apps

Here, make sure to create the same structure as you have in the payload in SAP Build Process Automation API trigger. definitionId will be ${API_TRIGGER_SBPA_DEFINITIONID} as mentioned in the API trigger.

After the process is trigger successfully, you can see the process within your SAP Build Process Automation tenant under Monitoring > Manage > Processes and Workflows tile.

TIP:

  • In case of any error, check network logs. They give the best information.

entry.title: Big News: A New Home for SAP Builders
entry.content:
Hi Builders, 

We have some exciting news to share! To provide you with a more connected experience, we are merging the SAP Builders group with the Application Development group—creating a central hub for all things application development and automation. 

What This Means for You 

  • The SAP Builders group will be merged into the Application Development group, which will be renamed Application Development and Automation to reflect its expanded focus. 
  • No action is needed on your part—you’ll automatically become a member of the new group. 
  • All existing blogs, discussions, and content from SAP Builders will be migrated to the new group, so nothing will be lost. 

This transition will happen on March 10, and we’ll keep you informed along the way. If you have any questions, feel free to ask in the comments—we’re here to help. 

Thanks for being part of this community. We’re excited for what’s ahead.

Stay tuned for more updates!

entry.title: Integration of Lottie with SAP Build Apps
entry.content:
Lottie Creator is a powerful web-based animation tool that simplifies motion workflow and offers a wide range of features. Import Lottie animations or SVGs, customize animations for your Mobile App or Web app

Lottie Useful links to prepare Animation

  • A powerful web-based Lottie animation tool
  • Featured Free Lottie Animations - Curated Motion Designs
  • Integrations and Plugins to Simplify Motion Design Workflow - LottieFiles
  • How to create a 3D flip card in Framer | LottieFiles for Framer - Tutorial - LottieFiles

Benefits of Using Lottie Animations

  • Enhanced User Experience:
    • Visual Appeal: Lottie animations can make your app more visually appealing and engaging
    • Interactivity: Adding animations can make the app feel more interactive and responsive
  • Performance:
    • Lightweight: Lottie animations are vector-based and lightweight, which helps in maintaining app performance
    • Scalability: They scale well on different screen sizes and resolutions without losing quality
  • Ease of Use:
    • Simple Integration: Lottie files are easy to integrate and use within SAP Build Apps.
    • Customization: They can be easily customized to fit the app's design and branding.

Step-by-Step Integration Guide in SAP Build Apps:

  • Setting Up: How to get started with SAP Build Apps and Lottie
  • Install Lottie from Marketplace :  How to find Lottie and Install
  • Adding Lottie Files: Instructions on importing Lottie JSON files into SAP Build Apps.
  • Using Lottie Components: How to use Lottie components within your app, including customization options

Follow the steps shown in numbers.. 

Preview the App

Use Cases

  • Web Applications:
    • Loading Animations: Use Lottie for loading screens to keep users engaged while content loads.
    • Interactive Elements: Enhance buttons, icons, and other interactive elements with subtle animations.
  • Mobile Applications:
    • Onboarding Screens: Use animations to make onboarding processes more engaging.
    • Feedback and Notifications: Provide visual feedback for user actions, such as form submissions or notifications.

Conclusion

  • Summary of Benefits: It makes great key benefits of integrating Lottie with SAP Build Apps.
  • Future Potential: We can briefly discuss the potential future developments and enhancements in this integration and much more to look beautiful animations without any coding or uploading JSON files into SAP Build Apps server side.

By integrating Lottie animations with SAP Build Apps, developers can create more dynamic and engaging applications that stand out in terms of both functionality and aesthetics. 

Keep sharing some creative ideas if you have anything for SAP Build Apps.

entry.title: Your Path to SAP Build Certification: Courses, Exam Prep, and Expert Tips
entry.content:
Earning an SAP certification is a great way to upskill, validate your expertise, and advance your career. For those working with SAP Build, there are two key certifications to consider: 

  • SAP Certified Associate - Low-Code/No-Code Developer - SAP Build (focused on SAP Build Apps, Process Automation, and Work Zone for app development and automation). 
  • SAP Certified Associate - SAP Build Work Zone - Implementation and Administration (focused on configuring and managing SAP Build Work Zone for digital workplace experiences). 

If you’re looking to get certified, this guide will walk you through both options—covering required courses, exam details, study tips, and resources to help you prepare. 

 

About the Exam 

Information about the exams, including the number of questions, cut scores and sample questions are published on learning.sap.com/certifications. The two available certifications for SAP Build are: 

  1. SAP Certified Associate - Low-Code/No-Code Developer - SAP Build  
  1. SAP Certified Associate - SAP Build Work Zone - Implementation and Administration  

 

The certification website gives you a breakdown of topic areas covered in each exam and their weight, so you know what to focus on.  

 

 

The certifications are valid for 12 months. You can extend the certification by 12 more months by completing an assessment. You will receive a heads up from SAP to let you know when it’s time to renew your certification. 

 

How to Schedule the Exam 

You can schedule your SAP Build certification exam in two ways: 

If You Have a Learning Hub Subscription 

With an SAP Learning Hub subscription, you receive four exam attempts per year as part of your plan. You can use these attempts to take up to four different certification exams or to retake an exam if needed. 

To schedule your exam: 

  1. Log in to learning.sap.com. 
  2. Find the certification exam you want to take. 
  3. Click Schedule. This will take you to the Questionmark certification portal, where you can view and manage your exam dates. 
  4. On the Questionmark certification portal, click Exam dashboard from the top-level navigation. Then click Schedule an exam.  
  5. Find the certification exam you want to take from the list (there are a lot, and it’s easier to do a control / command + F). Click the calendar icon to select the desired date and time. Then click Save Appointment. 
  6. To view your scheduled exams, click Exam Appointments from the top-level navigation.  

 

If You Don’t Have a Learning Hub Subscription 

If you don’t have a Learning Hub subscription, you can purchase a set of certification attempts.  

You have three purchase options: 

  • One certification attempt, available to purchase here. 
  • Two certification attempts, including 10 hours of practice system access with it, available to purchase here. 
  • A bundle of six attempts (usable for different certifications or exam retakes if needed), available to purchase here. 

Once purchased, follow the scheduling instructions on the website to book your exam. 

 

How to Stay Certified 

SAP Build certifications are valid for one year. To maintain your certification, you’ll need to renew it annually. 

  • If you have a Learning Hub subscription: You can renew your certification by completing a short assessment covering the latest SAP Build innovations. 
  • If you don’t have a Learning Hub subscription: You must purchase a certification attempt and retake the full exam. You can do so here. 

For more details, visit the SAP certification renewal guide. 

 

What to Study for the SAP Build Certification: Learning Paths 

The best way to prepare for the exam is to take learning paths. Completing the learning paths along with their quizzes and hands on exercises will provide the knowledge and practice needed to pass the certification. 

Below are the most relevant learning paths for each certification. 

SAP Certified Associate - Low-Code/No-Code Developer - SAP Build Exam 

If you're new to SAP Build, start with this introductory learning path: 

  • Composing and automating with SAP Build the No-Code Way 

To fully prepare for the certification exam, you’ll also need to complete the following learning paths: 

  • Developing Apps with SAP Build Apps using Drag-and-Drop Simplicity  
  • Creating Processes and Automations with SAP Build Process Automation  
  • Designing SAP Build Work Zone  
  • Experiencing End-To-End SAP Build  

These courses cover all required exam topics and must be completed before taking the certification. 

Please note that in order to take the SAP Certified Associate - Low-Code/No-Code Developer - SAP Build exam, you must complete the hands on portion of the Experiencing End-To-End SAP Build learning path. The proctor during the exam will ask for proof of completion. It’s a good idea to have a screenshot of your work handy.   

 

SAP Certified Associate - SAP Build Work Zone - Implementation and Administration 

If you are new to SAP Build, begin with:  

  • Composing and automating with SAP Build the No-Code Way 

For in-depth preparation specific to SAP Build Work Zone, complete the learning path below. This learning path covers deployment, system administration, integration, and extensibility options for SAP Build Work Zone. 

  • Implementing and Administering SAP Build Work Zone 

 

Additional Resources to Support Your Learning 

While the learning paths cover all the important topics required to pass your certification exam, it’s always helpful to reinforce your learning with additional resources and hands-on practice. Here are some valuable materials to deepen your understanding of SAP Build and boost your confidence before the exam. 

2 Minutes of SAP Build 

This YouTube playlist, created by Daniel Wroblewski, offers quick, digestible videos covering key SAP Build concepts. It’s a great way to reinforce your learning in just a few minutes. 

Live Sessions – SAP Build 

Join expert-led live sessions on SAP Build to get hands-on guidance, ask questions, and stay updated on the latest developments. See the upcoming live sessions here. You need the Learning Hub subscription to access these sessions.  

SAP CodeJams 

Participate in free CodeJam sessions, where you can practice SAP Build in a collaborative, hands-on environment. These sessions are ideal for gaining more experience and troubleshooting with experts. More info here.

SAP Build Missions & Tutorials 

Explore interactive missions on the SAP Developers site. These step-by-step guides will help you apply what you’ve learned and build confidence before taking the certification exam. 

 

Your SAP Build Exam Prep Checklist 

Follow these steps to maximize your chances of passing the exam on your first attempt. 

  1. Understand the exam structure.  
    • Review the certification webpage to learn about the exam topics and their weight, so you know where to focus your studies. 
    • Familiarize yourself with the exam format, including the number of questions, time limit, and passing score. 

  2. Follow the recommended learning journey 
    • Complete all the recommended learning paths outlined in this post. 
    • Make sure to complete all hands-on activities and quizzes to reinforce your understanding. 

  3. Get more hands-on experience 
    • Practice using SAP Build in different scenarios. For example, challenge yourself to create a team onboarding app, or a budget approval process.  
    • Experiment with SAP Build features beyond the course exercises to deepen your understanding. 

  4. Create a study plan 
    • Set a realistic study schedule. Spread your learning over a few weeks rather than cramming at the last minute. 

  5. Take a Practice Test  
    • Use the sample questions provided by SAP to get a feel for the exam format and question style. 

  6. Rest well before the exam 
    • Avoid last-minute cramming. It’s better to be well-rested and focused. 

  7. Read Questions Carefully During the Exam 
    • Pay attention to keywords like "best," "most effective," or "first step." 
    • Eliminate obviously wrong answers before choosing the best one. 
    • If unsure, mark the question for review and return to it later. 

 

What’s Next After Getting Certified 

Earning your SAP Build certification is a significant accomplishment, and your experience can be invaluable to others preparing for the exam. Share your journey in the Application Development and Automation Group by posting about your study approach, the resources that helped you most, and any tips you found useful. Whether it’s a hands-on exercise that deepened your understanding or a study habit that kept you on track, your insights can support and inspire fellow learners on their path to certification.

entry.title: How to Learn SAP Build: A Beginner’s Guide
entry.content:
If you're new to SAP Build and wondering where to start, this guide will help you navigate the best resources to gain hands-on experience and build your skills. Whether you’re looking for structured learning, interactive challenges, or live expert guidance, this guide will set you on the right path. 

 

Learning Paths 

The best place to start is the learning paths. SAP offers structured learning paths that guide you step-by-step through SAP Build concepts. These courses include interactive lessons and exercises to help you master the platform. Most importantly, they include a link to a free learning environment where you can practice your skills hands on.  

Recommended Learning Paths: 

If you're new to SAP Build, start with this introductory learning path: 

  • Composing and automating with SAP Build the No-Code Way 

Then, explore these specialized learning paths to deepen your knowledge in specific areas: 

  • Developing Apps with SAP Build Apps using Drag-and-Drop Simplicity  
  • Creating Processes and Automations with SAP Build Process Automation  
  • Designing SAP Build Work Zone  
  • Implementing and Administering SAP Build Work Zone 
  • Creating Applications and Extensions using SAP Build Code 
  • Experiencing End-To-End SAP Build 

 

SAP Build Missions & Tutorials 

Take advantage of interactive missions on the SAP Developers site. These step-by-step guides will help you apply your knowledge and build confidence before taking a certification exam.  

Check out the latest SAP Build missions here. 

 

Live Sessions – SAP Build 

Join expert-led live sessions for beginner sessions and deeper dive content, ask questions and stay updated on the latest SAP Build developments.  

See the upcoming live sessions here. Please note, you need the Learning Hub subscription to access these sessions.

 

2 Minutes of SAP Build 

Looking for quick, digestible learning content? This YouTube playlist offers bite-sized videos covering key SAP Build concepts. It’s a great way to reinforce your knowledge in just a few minutes. 

Click here to watch the videos. 

 

SAP CodeJams 

Host or participate in SAP CodeJam sessions to practice SAP Build in a collaborative, hands-on environment. These events are perfect for gaining experience, troubleshooting challenges with experts, and networking with fellow learners. 

More info here. 

 

Hands-On Challenges 

Challenge yourself with hands-on challenges created by SAP Developer Advocates. These challenges help solidify your learning while making the process fun and engaging. 

See previous challenges here. 

 

Next Steps: Earn Your SAP Build Certification 

An SAP Build Certification is a great way to prove your knowledge and expertise in low-code/no-code development. Whether you’re looking to advance in your current role or break into a new one, certification can give you the competitive edge you need. 

Find out more about SAP Build certification here. 

 

Keep Building Your SAP Skills 

No matter where you are in your learning journey, SAP Build offers a variety of resources to help you succeed. Start with structured learning paths, engage with the community through interactive challenges, and take advantage of live sessions to deepen your expertise. Happy learning!

entry.title: SAP Builders, Welcome to the Application Development and Automation Group!
entry.content:
Big news, Builders! 

The SAP Builders group is now part of the Application Development and Automation group, bringing together even more application developers, automation enthusiasts, and low-code/no-code innovators into one thriving community. 

Why the Change? 

We’re combining forces to give you a stronger, more vibrant space for all things SAP Build Apps, SAP Build Code, SAP Build Process Automation, SAP Build Work Zone, and beyond. This means more discussions, more resources, and a broader scope for your development journey. 

What Should You Do Now? 

  • If you were an SAP Builders group member, this is now your go-to place. 
  • Join the conversation! Ask questions, share insights, and keep the Builder mindset alive. 
  • Stay tuned for upcoming events, learning tips, and exclusive challenges designed just for you. 

This is just the beginning—we’re excited to continue growing and learning together! Let us know in the comments of this post what topics, resources, or activities you’d love to see in this newly expanded community. 

Welcome home, Builders!

entry.title: Webinar: Beyond Integration - How PepsiCo Builds Custom HR Applications with SAP BTP
entry.content:
Many organizations have a need to build custom HR applications and extensions to complement their SAP SuccessFactors solutions. As part of Comentec’s “SAP BTP for HR” webinar series, this episode moves beyond integration to explore the SAP Business Technology Platform's full potential for HR solutions.

Please join SAP BTP and SAP SuccessFactors partner, Comentec and PepsiCo's HR Technology team to demonstrate how SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) enables HR and HRIS to efficiently create high-quality custom HR extensions with limited development effort.

Using a Talent Card application as an example, Comentec will showcase how BTP's built-in templates, standards, and security features are combined to create robust solutions that HR technology teams can easily maintain while PepsiCo will share their firsthand experience of using BTP to develop a secure, sustainable HR application that utilizes data from SAP SuccessFactors.

When:  Date: April 2, 2025. |  Time: 11:30AM US EST, 17:30 CET
Registration Link

Join us to discover how SAP BTP's standardized components and best practices can help you:

  • Develop custom applications for SAP SuccessFactors with reduced effort and complexity
  • Create solutions that are easier to maintain and support internally
  • Leverage SAP's standard technology stack for improved sustainability
  • Build a stronger business case for custom application development

This session will help you reimagine the possibilities of BTP and provide you with the confidence to evaluate how it can be leveraged across your project roadmap.

This webinar is designed for:

  • Strategic decision-makers evaluating Employee Central and extensibility options
  • Technical leaders managing existing HR environment seeking modernization strategies
  • Business stakeholders wanting to improve data access, user experience, and HR processes.

Event info:
Date: April 2, 2025.
Time: 11:30AM US EST, 17:30 CET
Registration Link- this will take you to the registration page hosted by Comentec.

SAP Build Apps SAP Build Code SAP Business Technology Platform

entry.title: Joule for Developers: Word Search Challenge
entry.content:
Joule for developers is here, and we’ve hidden the key capabilities in this Word Search Challenge. The first capability you spot is the one you’re destined to explore! Will it be Joule Copilot, Code Explain or another game-changing feature? 

Take a quick look at the word search below and see which Joule for developers capability jumps out at you.  

 

How Do I Participate? 

  1. Sign into your community account (if you don't have one, create an account first). 
  2. Join the Application Development and Application Group if you're not a member yet. 
  3. Take a look at the Joule for developers word search. 
  4. Spot the first Joule for developers capability that cashes your eye. 
  5. Scroll down to the bottom of this post and click Reply.  
  6. Comment on the contest thread with: 
    • The Joule for developers capability you found first 
    • How you envision to use that capability and to what advantage 

That’s it! You’re in the running to win! 

What’s In It For You? 

Not only will you discover the Joule for developers capability you’re destined to use, but you’ll also have the chance to win a €40 SAP merchandise voucher. 25 lucky participants will be randomly selected – so trust your instincts, find your AI match, and join the fun! 

Curious to learn more about the Joule for developers capabilities? Check out the resources below:

  • Unlock new levels of productivity with AI in SAP Build
  • Joule for Developers FAQ: Features, Access and More

 

1. Challenge definition 

The " Joule for Developers Word Search Challenge" Competition 2025 with SAP Community is an online competition taking place in SAP Community (community.sap.com) where the announcement of the competition is being published as a contest in SAP Community Groups. Valid submissions need to be submitted as described in this announcement where every interested individual SAP Community member can participate in writing how they are excited to use an AI feature. Participation in the competition is subject to these Official Rules. The winner of this competition will be receiving one SAP merchandise voucher. The promoter of this challenge is the SAP Community team. 

2. Prize draw entry period details 

Eligible individuals may participate in the " Joule for Developers Word Search Challenge” Competition 2025 with SAP Community from March 18, 2025, at 3 PM to April 30, 2025, at 12:00 PM Central European Time. 

3. Eligibility Criteria 

Entrants must (i) be eighteen (18) years or older; and (ii) be a registered and active member of the SAP Community (community.sap.com). The Competition is subject to all federal, state, and local laws and is void where prohibited. Participants in the contest agree to be bound by these Official Rules and the decisions of the SAP Community team. Employees of SAP Community Team and its family members (parent, children, siblings, regardless of where they reside) and any person in any way affiliated with or related to government entities, government officials or any other person or entity in the public sector are ineligible. Your post must not: (a) violate any third party rights, including, but not limited to, copyrights, trademark rights, or rights of privacy and publicity; (b) depict any risky or offensive behavior or contain content that are obscene, defamatory, profane, offensive, lewd, pornographic, false, misleading, or deceptive; (c) prominently feature any trademarks or logos; or (d) disclose any confidential information or trade secrets as the stories will be published and promoted online. Sponsor reserves the right to reject any story for any reason. By submitting a post, you represent and warrant that: (a) the post is an original work created solely by you; (b) you own all rights to the post; and (c) the post and any comments you provide with the story comply with all requirements of these Official Rules. 

You may not participate if doing so violates any policies within your organization. If you are unsure whether your participation may violate any policies, we strongly encourage you to seek advice from your/an ethics or compliance official. Per SAP’s internal policies, public sector employees and government officials are not eligible to participate. Participation is your attestation that you are not a public sector employee or government official. 

4. Competition Rules – submission requirements 

Posts submitted must: 

- be uploaded during the contest entry period (see above) 

- be only submitted once (only one entry per person is allowed). 

5. Selection and Notification of Winner 

One can win this contest by being randomly selected from all eligible entries submitted during the contest entry period. The five selected winners will be notified via SAP Community Direct Message. 

6. Prize and Corresponding Approximate Retail value (ARV) 

One (1) SAP merchandise voucher with the value of 40 euro. Prize is not redeemable for cash. No substitution allowed except that promoter may, at its sole discretion, substitute a prize with another of equal or greater value. All taxes are solely the responsibility of the winner. 

7. Governing Law and Limits on Recovery 

This Competition shall be governed by and interpreted under the laws of Germany without regard to its conflicts of laws provisions. Any claims, judgments and/or awards shall be limited to actual out-of-pocket costs associated with entering this Competition. Participant hereby waives any rights or claims to legal fees, indirect, special, punitive, incidental, or consequential damages of participant, whether foreseeable or not and whether based on negligence or otherwise. 

8. Personal information and privacy 

By participating in the competition, participant(s) consent(s) to the collection, use and disclosure of participant’s personal information. For the name of the winner, an announcement will be published in the SAP Community Group ‘Application Development’. 

By participating in the competition, participant(s) consent(s) to the publication of the submitted story on the SAP Community site, and we may also use the submitted story for a community contest summary and as part of blog posts after the competition period.

entry.title: Joule for Developers FAQ: Features, Access and More
entry.content:
This FAQ provides an overview of Joule for developers, the AI-powered capabilities available in SAP Build and ABAP. It covers key features, access details, and how these tools support development and automation.

What is Joule for developers? 

Joule for developers is the collective name for the design-time AI capabilities across SAP Build, including ABAP, that help improve developer productivity and proficiency. These comprise of new and existing AI capabilities that are enabled by Joule within SAP Build Code, SAP Build Apps, SAP Build Process Automation and ABAP in SAP BTP and S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition. These Joule capabilities for developers help them build faster, code smarter, and automate better. 

Who is Joule for developers for? 

Joule for developers primarily enables developers, who want to create and/or customize business applications and automate business processes with SAP Build more efficiently. Joule capabilities for developers are centered around AI assistance for design-time needs for these apps or extensions. 

How does Joule for developers benefit customers? 

Joule for developers helps:  

  1. Drive efficiency across diverse development needs with centralized, AI-powered tools for app development and automation 
  2. Enhance productivity with seamlessly integrated AI development tools—no context switching needed. 
  3. Unlock precise, specialized outcomes with secure, purpose-built SAP models designed for your development needs. 

What are the capabilities of Joule for developers? 

Joule for developers offers a range of AI-powered capabilities to enhance developer productivity: 

  • Code generation and optimization:  Joule for developers can generate new code, autocomplete existing code, review, and refactor code for improved quality. 
  • Code understanding: Joule for developers can improve code understanding with code explanation, predictive code completion, code search and comment generation. 
  • Full-stack application development: Joule for developers assists in generating full-stack applications based on Cloud Application Programming Models and ABAP Core Data Services (ABAP CDS), as well as (unit) tests, and application logic. 
  • Automation workflow productivity: Joule for developers enhances automation workflows by summarizing processes and business rules, as well as generating and editing processes, forms, applied business rules, and scripts. 

What specific functionalities does Joule for developers provide in SAP Build? 

Below is a breakdown of the specific functionalities available in SAP Build Apps, SAP Build Code, SAP Build Process Automation and ABAP Environment. 

SAP Build Apps: 

  1. Generate pages to handle data entities 
  2. Generate sample data automatically (available in Q1/2025) 
  3. Generate formulas and translations (available in Q1/2025) 

SAP Build Code: 

  1. Inline Code Completion 
  2. Sample Data Generation for CAP 
  3. Unit Test Generation for CAP 
  4. Code Explain 
  5. Code Search 
  6. Code Comment Generation 
  7. CAP Application Generation 
  8. Business Logic Generation 
  9. Generate page metadata, action metadata, i18n content for MDK applications 
  10. Support UI5 freestyle application development 
  11. Code Refactoring 
  12. Generate SQL code from natural language 
  13. Support complex multi-step code for CAP  
  14. Generate testable Fiori elements app 
  15. Add UI to full-stack application 

SAP Build Process Automation: 

  1. Process and Business Rule (decision) Summarization 
  2. Processes, Forms, Business Rules, and Scripts Generation 

ABAP Environment: 

  1. Predictive Code Completion 
  2. ABAP Unit Test Generator 
  3. CDS Unit Test Generator 
  4. CDS Code Explain 
  5. ABAP Code Explain 
  6. RAP Business Object Generator 

The capabilities are available, unless otherwise stated. 

I am a developer. How can I access Joule for developers?  

For a limited time, we're offering free access to Joule capabilities for developers using SAP Build and ABAP Development Tools.

  • SAP Build: Free access is available until June 30, 2025.
  • ABAP Development Tools: Developers can sign up for the free offer until August 15, 2025, and continue using it at no cost until September 15, 2025.

The only requirement is that the organization or user must sign the SAP AI Terms.

Here's how you can access the Joule capabilities for developers across the various development tools: 

  • SAP Build Code: It is enabled by default if your sign-up for the service. 
  • SAP Build Process Automation: You have the option to enable the AI capabilities from the Build lobby. This will require you to read and agree to the SAP AI Terms. Once you confirm, the AI capabilities will become available for use. 
  • SAP Build Apps: You have the option to enable the AI capabilities from the Build lobby. This will require you to read and agree to the SAP AI Terms. Once you confirm, the AI capabilities will become available for use. 
  • ABAP environment: For ABAP AI capabilities you will need to sign up for a no-cost service SKU called 'Joule for developers, ABAP AI capabilities'. Again, this just ensures you understand the SAP AI Terms and agree to them. Once you enable this service in your BTP global account, the AI capabilities are made available for use. If you need help adding this service to your contract, please reach out to your SAP account team, and they can help you get setup. 

I am a partner. How can I access Joule for developers? 

We're in the process of enabling TDD license for Joule capabilities for developers. Until then partners can also access the free offer available now through the end of June 2025 (September 15, 2025 for ABAP) and utilize the capabilities.

entry.title: Unlock new levels of productivity with AI in SAP Build
entry.content:
Why do we need more AI in SAP Build?

For a very simple reason: our users want to be even more efficient in delivering innovations and SAP extensions. Our customers want to accelerate application development and process automation with the help of AI to get more time to focus on what really matters instead of spilling time on tedious and repetitive tasks.

Let me outline some of the problems our customers describe:

  • “As a business process expert, I want to get assistance to automate my processes and critical workflow much faster”.
  • “I’m an application developer, and I'd like to stop spending time on tedious tasks like creating data models and sample data to test my apps. And if AI could help me create some basic JavaScript code, not rocket science, that would be great!”
  • “For a software developer like me, it would be great if we could efficiently code applications and concentrate on the value-add coding part, so I can deliver on my projects faster”

At SAP, we’re committed to delivering AI capabilities that make it even better to develop with SAP Build. Just a month ago, we announced the general availability of our Joule copilot for ABAP developers: now Joule speaks ABAP!  Today we proudly announced new Joule capabilities for developers. In the next few paragraphs, I will take you on a deep dive into the new capabilities you can leverage to benefit from the power of (generative) Artificial Intelligence for building applications or automating business processes.

First, we identified a few key themes where AI has a significant impact in the realm of app development and process automation:

  • Application Generation: Generate CAP & RAP applications from scratch including data models, sample data, unit tests and more
  • Code Optimization: Generate and optimize JavaScript, Fiori/UI5 code with predictive code completion, code search, and code explanation
  • Artifacts Generation: Generate and summarize complex artifacts such as processes, data types, scripts, decisions, as well as approval recommendations

All these capabilities significantly accelerate application development and process automation and – let’s be honest – make it more fun, also.

Now let’s explore together how AI will assist you across all SAP Build.

SAP Build Process Automation

The purpose of AI and generative AI capabilities in SAP Build Process Automation is very simple yet powerful: assisting our customers in the implementation of their process automations. For that, the design capabilities leverage generative AI to allow users to interactively generate and edit artifacts from natural language description, as well as generating summaries of existing artifacts.

With that, we create immediate value for our customers:

  • Building artefacts such as processes, forms and business rules, and automating processes with the help of AI based on natural language input help increase developer productivity, improve quality and enable further innovations.

  • Summarizing processes, workflows and complex artefacts such as Business Decisions (Rules) using natural business language enables a quicker handover and reduced time for documenting processes.

We also added two additional AI-based capabilities that make process automation better:

  • DOX Premium service is integrated with SAP Build bringing a paradigm shift in document processing automation world. Generative Al-based document understanding not only integrates cutting-edge natural language processing capabilities but also holds the promise of eliminating the necessity for the laborious and costly endeavor of template creation and maintenance.
  • SAP Task Center's intelligent approval recommendation capability enables faster and better decisions based on machine learning, streamlining operations, and improving efficiency with AI-driven insights based on patterns observed in other approval tasks.

To get a concrete view on how AI works in SAP Build Process Automation, watch our 7 minutes video demo on Intelligent Processing of Sales Orders with Enterprise Automation.

SAP Build Apps

There is also a new AI-based capability in our no-code development environment. In SAP Build Apps we now have UI generation from data. The UI of an application is often directly related to the structure of the data handled by the application, so AI can propose Us based on data. In the screenshot below you can see data entities that are part of the application – in this case, Books, Authors and Genres – and right next to these, the UI pages were created by AI.

In order to activate this Generative AI capability in SAP Build Apps, you have to 

  1. Click on the "Generative AI" tile in your SAP Build tenant configuration:

  2. Enable Generative AI feature with the toggle:



SAP Build Code

Many AI capabilities have been introduced during the last months in SAP Build Code. Let me share a recap with you:

Joule supports complex multi-steps tasks for advanced CAP application generation

As of early 2025, the optimal approach for professional developers working with Generative AI involves creating project skeletons that cover general requirements, followed by incremental enhancements. SAP Build Code Joule expands on this practice with multi-tasks support, allowing developers to use a single prompt to engage specialized agents for various tasks. By coordinating these agents, Joule creates more comprehensive project foundations, thereby streamlining the development process. This approach enables developers to focus more on refining and customizing their applications, improving productivity and efficiency.

As an example, when a developer asks Joule for assistance in creating an application, an execution plan is displayed. The complex requirements are broken down into subtasks to generate

  1. the data model
  2. the sample data
  3. the application logic

and we apply our best practices for each step. The developer can review the improved user requirements and enhance them manually, via the pencil button.

For more details, read our blog post SAP Build Code Joule Evolves: Unveiling Multi-Agent Support for Advanced CAP Application Generation.

Joule for mobile app development

Joule now also supports mobile app development in SAP Build Code: it uses mobile services, like MDK action generation.

Find more in this blog post from Jitendra Kansal: “Streamline SAP MDK Development with the Power of Generative AI and Joule” and for additional details, read this technical documentation. 

Joule for searching and analyzing existing code

With Joule copilot, you can now use natural language queries to quickly and easily locate specific code snippets. You can simply ask Joule what you're looking for, and it will instantly show you the files relevant to your search criteria, saving you time and effort in your coding journey.

For more details, read our blog post Product Updates for SAP Build Code – October 2024 Edition.

Joule for SAP HANA Machine Learning

The SQL console provided by the SAP HANA Database Explorer extension in SAP Build Code supports SQL statement generation via natural language. Users can enter a text prompt and use the new run method “Run Prompt” to generate a SQL statement that can be executed on the SAP HANA Cloud database. The generated statement can be executed like any other statement directly from the SQL console.

For more details, read our blog post Product Updates for SAP Build Code – October 2024 Edition

Joule for Fiori tools and UI5 guided development

SAP Build Code also offers a variety of generative AI functionalities to support UI5 development. Joule can now be used to both accelerate the development and provide support for those who may not be UI5 experts.

For more details, read our blog posts UI5 freestyle development in SAP Build Code with Joule and SAPUI5 Meets TypeScript: A Smooth Migration Journey with Joule. 

The business user view: SAP Build Work Zone and AI

Of course, beyond the developer, we also investigate how we can make the SAP Build business user more efficient with the help of AI. So, while the below update is not part of our Joule for Developers capabilities, I still wanted to mention this and provide our readers the chance to dive deeper.

Joule is now integrated into SAP Build Work Zone Standard Edition, enhancing the workspace with AI-powered capabilities

This enables users to interact with Joule and get answers from all connected systems​. An example would be that you're trying to track the status of a purchase order. Instead of navigating through multiple screens, you simply ask Joule, “Has my purchase order been approved?”. Joule instantly provides the status and key details directly within its window in SAP Build Work Zone, this seamless interaction eliminates the need to switch between applications, saving time and effort.

For more examples of how Joule can transform your workspaces, read @Yumnahabbasi's blog post: “Joule is Now Integrated into SAP Build Work Zone!”

Feedback from Early Adopters: Up to 40% cost reduction!

Now, back to the developer view. We gathered feedback from beta users to understand the impact these capabilities have on their application development and process automation.  The results they achieved in terms of productivity speak for themselves:

  • Up to 40%​ cost reduction for ERP extension migration: it enables customers to review and transition existing extensions faster and more efficiently into the cloud. It simplifies future upgrades by providing side-by-side extensions that keep the ERP core clean
  • Up to 30% reduction in low-code process automation effort: building and automating processes based on natural language input, allows for increased developer productivity, improved quality and more innovations.
  • Up to 30%​ cost reduction for pro-code application development: generating code based on natural-language input and pre-trained code, and data generation model, allows for faster development, quicker migration to newer digital environments and improved development capacity for innovations.
  • Up to 10% time savings for process developers: summarizing processes and complex artefacts using natural language enables a quicker handover and reduced time for understanding processes.
  • Up to 10% faster time to market for new low-code applications: empowering developers to build applications and automate processes faster based on natural language input accelerates projects.

Join our Webcast

We've set up a webcast where I'll have the pleasure to be showing you AI in SAP Build in action, plus you'll have the chance to bring me and other product managers any questions you might have.

Join us on April 10, 2025!

  • APJ: 2:00 PM SGT (Singapore) / 4:00 PM AEST (Sydney)
  • EMEA: 2:00 PM CET (Central Europe) / 1:00 PM GMT (UK)
  • NA: 2:00 PM EST / 11:00 AM PST (Pacific)

You can also

  • Watch our Joule for Developers video
  • Attend our LinkedIn Live event on March 25th at 9AM PST
  • Participate in our SAP Community contest 
  • Check out the official FAQs on Joule for developers
  • Attend our SAP Community Ask Me Anything session in April (link to come soon)

Enjoy AI and Joule in SAP Build!

entry.title: New Learning Journey Extending SAP Sales Cloud and SAP Service Cloud Version 2 using SAP Build Apps
entry.content:
Hello SAP CX Community! This is Fernando speaking!

During the last few years, we've been producing learning assets for SAP Sales and Service Cloud version 1 extensibility topics. This year though we plan to focus on version 2 extensibility and integration topics. So, today we're very proud to announce in this blogpost, the next learning milestone for those who want to master the extensibility of SAP Sales and Service Cloud version 2 using SAP Build Apps.

Please join me in welcoming to our SAP's learning platform, this new learning journey: 


New Learning Journey 
Extending SAP Sales Cloud and SAP Service Cloud Version 2 using SAP Build Apps

If you need to get ready to extend SAP Sales and Service Cloud version 2 using SAP Build Apps as a citizen developer, you’ve come to the right place. You'll grow the hands-on knowledge that will help you to run end to end the extensibility project. And there is a plus, you'll also learn how to include generative AI capabilities from SAP Core AI in this course.

We've curated our content so that after completing this learning journey, developers, administrators, and consultants will gain the skills to be able to setup, configure and implement the extensibility between SAP SAP Sales and Service Cloud version 2 using SAP Build Apps and SAP Core AI.

Here you have a glimpse of how it looks:

 

Audience
The course is meant to be helpful for:

  • Technical Consultants
  • Administrators
  • Developers
  • Architects

Learning Objectives
Upon the completion of this course, a Consultant or Developer will be able to extend SAP Sales Cloud and SAP Service Cloud Version 2 using SAP Build Apps and SAP AI Core Services to customize the solution to their specific business needs.

Learning Content
The learning journey consists of 3 units and provides 3 hours of content:

  • Extending SAP Sales Cloud and SAP Service Cloud Version 2 with SAP Build Apps: An introduction to the features and limitations of Side-by-Side extensibility and how to establish the connection between SAP Sales Cloud and SAP Service Cloud Version 2 and SAP Build Apps.
  • Designing an Extension App with SAP Build Apps to Extend SAP Sales Cloud and SAP Service Cloud Version 2
    This unit guides you:
    • Create a new project in SAP Build Apps.
    • Design and preview the user interface of the extension app.
    • Integrate the data from SAP Sales Cloud and SAP Service Cloud Version 2 with the connected extension app.
    • Integrate the SAP AI Core service with SAP Build Apps to enable the AI-based generation and translation of e-mail texts in the extension app.
    • Deploy the Extension App.
  • Embedding and Testing the Extension App in SAP Sales Cloud and SAP Service Cloud Version 2
    This unit walks you through:
    • Create a mashup to display the extension app within the SAP Sales Cloud and SAP Service Cloud Version 2 user interface.
    • Log into SAP Sales Cloud and SAP Service Cloud Version 2 to ensure the embedded app works as expected.

After completing successfully this Learning Journey you'll be granted with a digital badge like this one which could be shared and linked to your social networks:

 

 

 

 

 

Give it a try! We plan to create more and more extensibility learning content for SAP Sales and Service Cloud version 2, and we'd like you to go over the lessons and let us know if they are useful for you or your team.

Additional Resources
There is a related live session for In-App extensibility for SAP Sales Cloud and SAP Service Cloud version 2. You can find more free learning journeys at the SAP Learning site for Integration and Extesibility, where you can build in-demand SAP skills for variety of topics specifically the SAP CX Solutions portfolio. If you are more in for quick video learning, then, look for our videos Integration and Extensibility videos for SAP Customer Experience to get updates on the latest integration and extensibility videos for SAP Customer Experience. solutions.

entry.title: 🎉SAP Build Brings Generative AI to Process Automation to enable your Business Processes
entry.content:
Try now in your SAP BTP Trial, Free Tier, Global Account - SAP Build Generative AI

 

Enabling Generative AI

Enable Generative AI in Control Tower for automated recommendations, enhanced efficiency, and time savings. Use it to create and summarize complex business processes, analyze data for informed decisions, and elevate app development. 

Business Context

SAP Build offers the following Generative AI functionality to enhance your business operations. You have the flexibility to enable or disable the Generative AI feature at the tenant level.
  • Automatically Create and Summarize Complex Business Processes: Outline workflows, generate step-by-step instructions, and ensure clear and efficient operations.

  • Use AI to Analyze Data and Generate Informed Decisions: Create a summary of decisions, get action recommendations actions, provide insights, and enable better decisions across your organization.

  • Streamline Your Data Collection with AI-Generated Forms: Automatically create forms tailored to your specific needs, ensuring accuracy and saving you time creating and managing forms.
  • Elevate Your App Development Process with AI-Driven UI Generation: Get help to create user-friendly interfaces with layout, component, and style suggestions, helping you build applications faster and more efficiently.

How to enable Generative AI in SAP Build

  1. From the left navigation menu, choose Control Tower.
  2. In the Tenant Configuration, choose Generative AI.

  3. Use toggle switch to enable Generative AI and accept the AI Usage Terms and Conditions.

SAP Build Process Automation - Usage of Generative AI

You can now use generative artifical intelligence in SAP Build Process Automation to generate and edit business processes, generate business rules, generate new code in a scipt task, and generate certain artifacts.

You can use generative AI to do the following:
  • Generate a business process, a data type, or a form from the Overview screen of your project. See Generate a Data Type and Generate a Form.
  • Edit or add steps to a process. See Generate a Process.
  • Generate new code in a script task. See Generate a Script using Generative AI in a Script Task
  • Explain a decision or generate business rules. See Use Generative AI in a Decision.

To use these features you need to enable generative AI for your tenant. See Generative AI.

The generative AI features are only supported on the following data centers:
  • Americas - US10, US30
  • EMEA - EU10, EU11, EU20
  • Asia and Australia - AP10

Note that some restrictions and limitations apply.

Known Limitations for Generative AI

When using generative AI, note the following limitations:
  • You can only use generative AI in the following cases:
    • You can generate a business process and explain a business process. It is also possible to edit a process and add some process steps. Note that it is not possible to generate branches, go-to steps, or map data.
    • You can generate a form
    • You can generate a data type
    • You can generate new code in a script task
    • You can explain a decision and generate business rules
  • Artifacts that are not listed here are not supported.
  • You cannot edit the content of artifacts, with the exception of the process.
  • You cannot release, deploy or delete a process using generative AI.
  • When using generative AI in the script editor, you can only generate code using JavaScript version ES 5.1.

Generative AI

Area Resource Quota
Tenant LevelAI requests for proceses1000 / month
 AI requests for business rules1200 / month

Generative AI - Model Limits

Element Name Property Name Limit
Process or ArtifactPrompt Description5 to 1000 characters
ProcessProcess Summarization

100 nodes

500 links between nodes

Restrictions, Quotas, Limits - Reference Documentation

entry.title: How to Enable Joule Capabilities in SAP Build Process Automation and SAP Build Apps
entry.content:
SAP recently announced some new Joule capabilities for developers within SAP Build Process Automation and SAP Build Apps. If you’re wondering how to enable these capabilities in your workspace, you’re in the right place.

In this post, we’ll look at the steps to undertake to enable the AI capabilities. But first, let’s just recap the announcement and the various recent posts:

  • Joule for developers, announced on March 18, is a set of AI capabilities enabled by Joule across SAP Build and ABAP developer tools.
  • Read the announcement blog here
  • Read the technical blog here
  • Read the FAQs on Joule for developer here

Now, lets look at the steps to enable the AI capabilities in SAP Build:

1. On the Build homepage, click Control Tower in the left-hand navigation panel.

2. Select Generative AI from the settings canvas.

3. Toggle the Enable AI button

 

4. Read and accept the SAP AI terms

5. Verify that the AI features are enabled

Availability and Licensing

Joule capabilities for developers are currently available across SAP Build Code, ABAP, SAP Build Process Automation, and SAP Build Apps.

Good news! Now through the end of June, we’re making Joule capabilities for developers accessible for free to all developers using SAP Build and ABAP Development Tools. For all SAP Build development tools, this free period extends until the end of June 2025. The ABAP AI capabilities will be available for free until August 2025. The only requirement to take advantage of this free offer is for the organization/user to sign the SAP AI Terms. Once this free offer concludes, the commercial model based on AI Units will take effect.

Note: You will still need the license to use the underlying SAP Build solutions and ABAP to utilize the AI capabilities within those tools.

Start leveraging the power of AI today! Enable Joule for developers in your SAP Build Process Automation and SAP Build Apps environments and experience a significant boost in your development efficiency. Don't miss out on the free offer available for a limited time!

entry.title: Create SAP Build Apps applications in minutes with AI
entry.content:
Introduction

In this blog post we are covering the newly released AI feature to automatically create pages based on your existing OData backend with SAP Build Apps. This new feature provides a jump start into application development and enables you to easily extend your application based on the generated pages.

 

Prerequisites

  • Subscribed to SAP Build Apps either via your preferred payment model or trial
  • You have an existing backend based on OData, e.g., your connected SAP S/4HANA system

 

Use Case

In our example, we want to provide our field colleagues with some information about the product so that they have the information directly at hand once on the customer side.

 

Accessing SAP Build

The starting point for all SAP Build products is the lobby. In the lobby you have a holistic overview of all projects you have access to as well as additional functionalities such as accessing connectors, the SAP Build store, monitoring capabilities, and the control tower to execute additional configurations based on your tenant.

 

Enabling AI Features

To initially enable the AI features of SAP Build in your tenant, you need to enable these first. Within the lobby of SAP Build choose Control Tower on the left hand. Within the Tenant Configuration you see a tile called Generative AI. If you have chosen it, you will see a toggle to enable it. For more information about availability and licensing, please see here.

 

Creating an SAP Build Apps Frontend Project and Consuming your Backend Data

Back in the lobby, you can now proceed with creating a new SAP Build Apps frontend project.  

 

 

Once in the frontend editor, go to the Integrations tab.

 

Choose your desired backend by, e.g., SAP S/4HANA public cloud edition. Choose Install Integration on the top right and afterwards enable the data entity by choosing the button which appears afterwards under the Install Integration button.

 

Create pages using AI

Once installed and enabled, a new button called Generate Pages will be shown. This button is powered by AI and creates the relevant CRUD (create, read, update, delete) pages for you.

 

After a few seconds all relevant pages are automatically generated. Choose open pages view.

 

You will get forwarded to the frontend editor in which you see the overview of all pages. To view the page in detail, choose the relevant page, e.g., edit products.

 

Preview your application

To test your application, click Save, and then choose the Preview button.

The generated pages and application can now be easily enhanced based on your needs. Simply modify the generated content, copy and paste the pages, and add additional fields as you desire.

 

Current quota limits for this functionality

 
Currently, we do have a quota limit based on 100 requests / user / month. Please have a look into this documentation in case of any updates.

 

For more information

Unlock new levels of productivity with AI in SAP Build

Consuming SAP with SAP Build Apps - adding S/4HANA Cloud

Get started with SAP Build Apps with an SAP BTP Trial Account

entry.title: What’s New in SAP Build – Q1 2025 Release Highlights
entry.content:
SAP Build continues to evolve to support how modern teams work — now with more artificial intelligence, flexibility, and speed. In Q1 2025, we delivered a wave of innovations across SAP Build Code, Process Automation, Apps, and Work Zone to help developers and business users turn ideas into enterprise-ready solutions faster than ever.

Whether you're building custom apps, creating SAP S/4HANA extensions, or streamlining processes, these updates will help you move from concept to value more efficiently.  Let's review the top 10 updates we selected from last quarter.

Licensing and Availability Updates

1. SAP Task Center Now included with SAP Build Work Zone

SAP Task Center is now embedded in all SAP Build Work Zone editions, giving users a unified view of workflow tasks from multiple SAP systems — right within their Work Zone homepage. No separate integration is required. This update also extends to partner licensing, making including Task Center in custom Work Zone scenarios easier. The result is faster approvals, centralized task tracking, and higher productivity for business users and managers — all in one workspace.

R

2. SAP Build Apps Now Available in More Regions

You can now deploy SAP Build Apps in EU11 and Sydney data centers, bringing your applications to more users. The increased coverage improves app responsiveness, supports region-specific compliance needs, and enables global scalability for your enterprise use cases.

Joule for Developers Updates

3. Generate End-to-End Workflows with a Prompt

In SAP Build Process Automation, developers can now describe business scenarios in natural language — and let generative AI create forms, decisions, and process flows automatically. Powered by SAP's purpose-built large language models (LLMs), this capability delivers contextualized, SAP-aware outcomes that speed up workflow creation and reduce manual effort. It also empowers business users to contribute to process design without writing code.

4. Generate UI Pages from Data in SAP Build Apps

SAP Build Apps now uses generative AI to propose user interface pages based on your data model. For example, if your app contains entities like Books, Authors, or Genres, Joule recommends corresponding screens, layouts, and controls. This helps teams move quickly from data modeling to interactive UI design, shortening prototyping cycles and improving design consistency.

5. Use Generative AI for Fiori UI Creation in SAP Build Code

Developers can now use Joule in SAP Build Code to generate Fiori-based UIs for existing CAP apps by entering the command /add-ui or selecting the Joule icon in the Storyboard. Joule understands your application structure and proposes relevant layouts, controls, and navigation patterns, helping you scaffold front-end apps quickly and focus on customization instead of starting from scratch.

6. Multi-Agent Support for CAP App Generation

SAP Build Code now uses multi-agent support powered by Joule to generate complete CAP applications. Each agent focuses on a specific part — like data models, services, or UI — so you get a more complete project from a single prompt. This enhancement saves time and helps you focus on business logic instead of building everything from scratch.

Read also Overview of all GenAI Options in SAP Build Code by david_bizer.

Enhance SAP Business Suite Applications

7. Extend SAP S/4HANA with Custom UIs (Planned for Release Soon)

Coming very soon in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition, developers will be able to use the SAP Build Extensibility Wizard to create side-by-side extensions—either by customizing existing SAP Fiori apps or building entirely new web applications. This approach enables teams to meet specific business needs while preserving the core system, supporting clean core principles and long-term agility.

  • See documentation: Using the Extensibility Wizard.
  • On setup, read the blog SAP Build Extensibility Wizard for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition by @Karthik-Thiru. 

8. New Enhancements for Guided Experiences by SAP Build

New enhancements to Guided Experiences by SAP Build help simplify complex processes like employee onboarding, parental leave, procurement approvals, and IT service management, where multiple steps, systems, and stakeholders are involved. With this release, ready-to-use content packages for SAP SuccessFactors Employee Onboarding and Cross-boarding are now available in the SAP Build Store—enabling faster rollout of consistent, step-by-step guidance for new hires and internal transfers.

  • Read more: Taking SAP Build Guided Experiences to the next level by florian_buech.
  • Watch the 2-minute video: Navigating Parental Leave with SAP Build Guided Experiences.

9. Create a Wait for an API Call Trigger

With new support for intermediate message events, SAP Build Process Automation lets workflows pause until specific API-triggered inputs are received and resume automatically. This powerful capability reduces wait times and manual monitoring, helping teams design more adaptive and responsive processes with fewer errors and overhead. 

See documentation: Create a Wait for an API Call Trigger | SAP Help Portal

Boost Developer Productivity

10. Revamped Project Overview in SAP Build Code

A redesigned Project Overview in SAP Build Code provides a clean, tree-based view of all app components, supporting CAP Java and CAP Node. This enhanced layout integrates with the Storyboard, making it easier for developers to navigate complex projects, onboard new team members, and stay focused while building.

See documentation: Project Overview | SAP Help Portal

Ready to See These Updates in Action?

These are just a few of the top updates rolling out this quarter. Want to explore the new features in more detail, see live demos, and get your questions answered by product experts?

👉Join us on April 15 for the What's New in SAP Build Q1 2025 webinar.
Reserve your spot to discover these updates in action. Register below:

📅There are two options to cover different time zones.
🕘9:00 – 10:00 AM CET → https://sap.to/604200bfg
🕔5:00 – 6:00 PM CET → https://sap.to/604500bfc

Learn More

For a complete list of new features, check out the following resources:

  • SAP Build Help Portal
  • SAP Build Road Map Explorer

Please stay current and connect with fellow SAP Builders by joining our Application Development and Automation Community for the latest updates, activities, and challenges.

entry.title: Send multipart/form-data files with custom JavaScript
entry.content:
Guys, I've developed a JS code for sending multipart/form-data requests with a custom JavaScript function.

Make good use of it...

 

var fileData = inputs.fileData;
var key = inputs.apikey;
var Authorization = inputs.authorization;

async function postMultipartFormData(fileData) {
    try {
        const formData = new FormData();

        // Add the API key
        formData.append("key", key);  // Using the key received dynamically

        // Fetch the file via blob URL
        const responseBlob = await fetch(fileData.path);
        if (!responseBlob.ok) {
            throw new Error(`Error fetching the file: ${responseBlob.status} - ${responseBlob.statusText}`);
        }
        const blob = await responseBlob.blob();
        const mimeType = blob.type;  // Get the MIME type of the file

        // Create the file with the correct MIME type
        const file = new File([blob], fileData.name, { type: mimeType });

        // Add the file to FormData
        formData.append("file", file);  // Add the file with its correct MIME type

        // Custom headers
        const headers = {
            //"Authorization": `Bearer ${Authorization}`,  // Using the authorization token
            //"Another-Header": "header-value"             // Example of another header
        };

        // Make the request using fetch
        const response = await fetch("https://api.yoursite.com/upload", {
            method: "POST",
            body: formData,
            //headers: headers  // Include headers if needed
        });

        if (!response.ok) {
            throw new Error(`Request failed: ${response.status} - ${response.statusText}`);
        }

        // Check the response structure
        const responseData = await response.json();
        if (!responseData || !responseData.data) {
            throw new Error("API response does not contain the expected URL.");
        }

        return { result: responseData.data, erro: null };
    } catch (error) {
        console.error("Error uploading data:", error.message);
        return { result: null, erro: error.message };
    }
}

return postMultipartFormData(fileData).then(response => {
    return { result: response.result, erro: response.erro };
});

entry.title: SAP Developer News April 3rd, 2025
entry.content:
DESCRIPTION 

Podcast: https://podcast.opensap.info/sap-developers/2025/04/03/sap-developer-news-april-3rd-2025/ 

Developer Challenge 

  • Blog: TK 
  • SAP Community Escape House: https://sap-community-escape-house.cfapps.us10.hana.ondemand.com/house 

 Generative AI in SAP Build Process Automation  

  • Blog : https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sap-build-brings-generative-ai-to-process-automation-to-enable-your/ba-p/14049869  
  • SAP Help Portal : https://help.sap.com/docs/build-process-automation/sap-build-process-automation/generate-process?locale=en-US  

 Generative AI in SAP Build Apps 

  • Blog post https://community.sap.com/t5/application-development-and-automation-blog-posts/create-sap-build-apps-applications-in-minutes-with-ai/ba-p/14054862 
  • SAP Help portal https://help.sap.com/docs/build-apps/service-guide/generate-pages-using-ai 

 SAP BTP Usability Event – HANA Cloud 

  • Registration form: https://forms.office.com/Pages/ResponsePage.aspx?id=bGf3QlX0PEKC9twtmXka9zoyeoeObGdNh1zA5HmpzP5UMEVIN0xWUlBRWUlCWUlQUVJKV1pZMDFBWS4u  

  

CHAPTER TITLES  

0:00 Intro 

0:10 Developer Challenge 

1:12 Generative AI in SAP Build Process Automation 

2:08 Generative AI in SAP Build Apps 

3:08 SAP BTP Usability Event – HANA Cloud 

 

TRANSCRIPTION

[Intro] This is the SAP Developer News for April 3rd, 2025.

[Daniel] You might have heard the sound of heavy machinery working on an addition to the SAP community escape house. Well, I'm happy to say that the SAP build garage is now ready. April's Developer Challenge will deal with a specific and key feature of SAP build. Well, I can tell you that. You have to discover that for yourself. It will include spy gear, exercise equipment, something from last month's AI challenge, and a few things that you won't see when you first enter the room. It will be a real challenge, no doubt about it. But if you're able to get out of the garage alive, please add a comment to my blog post and tell me how it went. Good luck.

[Rekha DR] Hello everybody! Are you a developer using SAP Build tools to build applications and automations? Then here is a good news for you to amplify productivity and efficiency in your day-to-day work. You can now use Generative AI in SAP Build Process Automation. You can enable Generative AI for your BTP tenant within the control tower of SAP. and then you can use Generative AI to generate a business process, a data type or a form from the overview screen of the project. Within the process, you can create an automation task, explain the business process in natural language. Generate new code in the script task, explain a decision or generate business rules. For more details, check this blog post by product expert, Yogananda Muthaiah provided in the description box. Keep learning, keep exploring. Thank you.  

[Shrinivasan] SAP applications are continuing to evolve with generative AI capabilities, and these capabilities are now available in SAP Build Apps. There is a new AI powered feature available in our no-code development environment now. SAP Build Apps now includes a generative AI feature that makes it easier to create pages for create, read, update operations on you. your existing OData backup. Simply enable an identity from the Integrations tab, and you will see the new Generate Pages option. Keep in mind, you will need to enable the Generative AI feature from the Control Tower first to use this option. Once generated, these pages can be customized and extended further to suit your specific needs. Note that the generated Pages feature comes with a usage limit of 100 requests per month for each user. Explore this new functionality to to jumpstart your application development. The links to the blog post and the help portal on this new feature is available in the description.

[Antonio] Holla, SAP Database Administrators and Database Developers. Have you ever wanted to provide feedback on the SAP HANA Cloud database service? Well, now you can. There will be an SAP BTP usability event on the 13th and 14th of May in Walldorf Germany. This is an in-person event where you can gain insights on the SAP's product vision. Also, you can share ideas directly with the development teams and provide your input on new features directly to the engineering teams. If you're interested in participating, make sure to register on the link that I'm going to share in the description of this video. Thank you very much for your time.Ciao

entry.title: So this is it: I’m VERPed!
entry.content:
📢  Today is my last day working for SAP.

Indeed, I've been accepted into the very last round of the Voluntary Early Retirement Plan launched by SAP France in 2024, for which I applied the day of my 62nd birthday, on March 10.

It has been quick, but I'm happy. I thanks my management hierarchy Diana Roesner / André Borchert / Thomas Volmering / Steffen Pietsch / Michael Ameling / Muhammad Alam / Christian Klein (*) for giving me the unique opportunity to stop working 33 months earlier than expected! 😊

(*) yes, I was very low in SAP org chart 😉 but I do not care: what matters to me is to bring my expertise where and when it makes a real impact, and I humbly think it was the case during my 6.5-year tenure in this great company. 

I will really miss my wonderful team of such lovely colleagues in charge of SAP Build Product Management, Enablement and Communications 🥹  ... but I will still be hanging out here on SAP Community and on LinkedIn to follow what they will be doing with SAP Build and broadly SAP Business Technology Platform with Artificial Intelligence and Joule, because I know what will be announced in a few weeks at SAP Sapphire 2025, and for sure they will rock the stage!

 

I would like to heartfully thank Diana Roesner, André Borchert, Thomas Volmering, Michael Ameling, Sebastian Schrötel, Bhagat Nainani, Juergen Mueller and all the colleagues for having supporting me during my 6+ years at SAP, during this amazing journey from SAP Intelligent RPA to SAP Build. 🙏🏻

👩🏻‍🎓👨🏻‍🎓 I seize this opportunity to promote 4️⃣ learnings on SAP Business Technology Platform and SAP Build.

Indeed, I won't spoil anything but there will be tremendous announcements in a few weeks: leverage these learnings to be prepared!

✅ An Enterprise Architect’s View on SAP Business Technology Platform

✅ Experiencing End-To-End SAP Build

✅ Compose and Automate with SAP Build the No-Code Way

✅ Creating Processes and Automations with SAP Build Process Automation

📖  Let me also recommend the SAP Build Process Automation book from SAP PRESS!

Order this book now and learn how to create processes, automations, actions, and decisions by following step-by-step instructions with this expert-written guide by my dear colleagues @ArchanaShukla, @Chaitanya_Priya Puvvada, @Stephan Schluchter, Seshadri Sreenivas, and Steven Jacobson.

Finally, I would like to say that I'm very happy because I will spend 2 days in Paris April 14-15, 2 days in Walldorf May 12-13 and finally 2 days in Berlin May 14-15 to hang out with my colleagues and say a warm and grateful goodbye: if you will be there, come say hi! 🥲

And now, for me it's time to…

 

 💙 I wish you all the very best! 💙

entry.title: SAP Build 新功能：2025年一季度版本亮点
entry.content:
本文本为翻译文，原文档链接What’s New in SAP Build – Q1 2025 Release Highlights，原作者 Jesse Sukhbayar。

SAP Build 不断发展，利用更多人工智能、灵活性和速度，为现代团队的工作方式提供支持。2025 年第 1 季度，我们针对 SAP Build Code、Process Automation、Apps 和 Work Zone 推出了一系列创新成果，帮助开发人员和业务用户比以往更快地将创意转化为企业级解决方案。

无论您是构建自定义应用、创建 SAP S/4HANA 扩展还是简化流程，这些更新都将帮助您更高效地从概念过渡到价值。  我们来回顾一下从上一季度中选择的前 10 项更新。

 

许可和可用性更新

1.SAP Task Center 现在包含在 SAP Build Work Zone 中

SAP Task Center 现在嵌入在所有 SAP Build Work Zone 版本中，为用户提供来自多个 SAP 系统的工作流任务的统一视图 - 位于其 Work Zone 主页中。无需单独集成。此更新还扩展到合作伙伴许可，使自定义 Work Zone 场景中的 Task Center 更容易。这样，业务用户和管理人员就可以在一个工作区中加快审批速度，集中跟踪任务，并提高工作效率。

  1. SAP Build Apps 现在可在更多区域使用

现在，您可以在 EU11 和悉尼数据中心部署 SAP Build Apps，将应用程序提供给更多用户。增加的覆盖范围可提高应用的响应能力，支持区域特定的合规性需求，并为企业用例提供全局可扩展性。

 

Joule for Developers 

3.使用提示生成端到端工作流

在 SAP Build Process Automation 中，开发人员现在可以使用自然语言描述业务场景，并允许生成性 AI 自动创建表单、决策和流程流。此功能由 SAP 专门构建的大型语言模型 (LLM) 提供支持，可提供基于情境的 SAP 感知结果，从而加快工作流创建速度并减少手动工作量。它还使业务用户能够在无需编写代码的情况下对流程设计做出贡献。

4.从 SAP Build Apps 中的数据生成 UI 页面

SAP Build Apps 现在使用生成式 AI 根据您的数据模型建议用户界面页面。例如，如果您的应用包含 Books、Authors 或 Genres 等实体，Joule 会推荐相应的屏幕、布局和控件。这有助于团队快速从数据建模转变为交互式 UI 设计，缩短原型设计周期并提高设计一致性。

5.在 SAP Build Code 中使用 Generative AI 创建 Fiori UI

开发人员现在可以通过输入命令 /add-ui 或在故事板中选择焦耳图标，在 SAP 构建代码中使用 Joule 为现有 CAP 应用生成基于 Fiori 的 UI。焦耳了解您的应用程序结构，并建议相关的布局、控件和导航模式，帮助您快速使用脚手架前端应用，并专注于定制，而不是从头开始。

6.CAP 应用生成的多代理支持

SAP Build Code 现在使用由 Joule 支持的多代理支持来生成完整的 CAP 应用程序。每个代理都专注于特定部分（如数据模型、服务或 UI），因此您可以从单个提示中获取更完整的项目。此增强节省了时间，可帮助您关注业务逻辑，而不是从头开始构建所有内容。

另请阅读 SAP Build Code 中的所有 GenAI 选项概览 ， david_bizer .

 

增强 SAP Business Suite 应用程序

7.使用自定义 UI 扩展 SAP S/4HANA（计划即将发布）

即将在 SAP S/4HANA Cloud（公共版）中推出，开发人员将能够使用 SAP Build 可扩展性向导创建并排扩展 - 通过定制现有 SAP Fiori 应用或构建全新的 Web 应用程序。这种方法使团队能够满足特定的业务需求，同时保留核心系统，支持精简核心原则和长期灵活性。

  • 请参阅文档：使用可扩展性向导。
  • 在设置中，请阅读博客 @Karthik-Thiru 提供的 SAP Build Extensibility Wizard for SAP S/4HANA Cloud 公共版。 

8.SAP Build 引导式体验的新增强功能

SAP Build 对引导式体验进行了新的增强，有助于简化复杂的流程，如员工入职、育儿假、采购审批和 IT 服务管理，其中涉及多个步骤、系统和利益相关方。 在此版本中，现在可以在 SAP Build Store 中获取 SAP SuccessFactors Employee Onboarding 和转岗的即用型内容包，从而更快地为新员工和内部调动提供一致的逐步指导。

  • 开始： SAP Build 引导式体验 ，florian_buech
  • 观看视频（2 分钟）：借助 SAP Build 引导式体验，规划育儿假。

9.实时工作流的中间消息事件

通过对中间消息事件的新支持，SAP Build Process Automation 允许工作流暂停，直到自动接收并恢复特定 API触发的输入为止。这种强大的功能可减少等待时间和手动监控，帮助团队设计更具适应性和响应能力的流程，同时减少错误和开销。 

 

提高开发人员工作效率

10.SAP Build Code 中改进的项目概览

SAP Build Code 中重新设计的项目概览提供了所有应用组件的基于树的清洁视图，支持 CAP Java 和 CAP 节点。此增强的布局与故事板集成，使开发人员能够更轻松地导航复杂项目、启用新团队成员并在构建时保持专注度。

了解更多

有关新功能的完整列表，请查看以下资源：

  • SAP Build 帮助门户
  • SAP Build Road Map Explorer

entry.title: Setting up SAP Document Grounding for Chat Apps
entry.content:
Great news, SAP has released the grounding management in AI Launchpad. The grounding management app allows you to manage your data pipelines. You’re able to create a new data repository, connect the document store (e.g., SharePoint) and use the grounding management with the orchestration service.

As someone who enjoys learning by building, I saw this as the perfect opportunity to dive in. I wanted to understand how to setup and use AI Core document grounding, and build a simple chat app using SAP Build Apps to visualize the result. Just a fun, hands-on way to explore the tech and see what’s possible.
